category_title: Students who repeat the teacher's text to avoid reading new material
minimal_context: The student and interventionist are taking turns reading 'Miss Nelson is missing!' during a one-on-one session.
dialogue:
TUTOR: I will read this line: 'The kids were being rude or silly.' Now it is your turn to read.

STUDENT: Was rude or that one.

TUTOR: That's the part I read. Try the next line.

STUDENT: I just said... was rude or silly.

TUTOR: What's the next one say?

STUDENT: You's gonna read the next one. I read this one.

options.A: Gives a verbal warning about the behavior at the time but does not discipline the student further during the shared reading session.
options.B: Allows the student to withdraw from the shared reading session for a time-limited period (Quiet Time) to prevent an emotional overload.
options.C: Clarifies the reading invitation verbally and nonverbally by pointing to the next line of text and stating that it is the student's turn.
options.D: Provides opportunities to respond (OTRs) as a structured form of tutor attention during the shared reading session.
